jokes.text: Guy in t-shirt and jeans mistaken for Johnny Depp - 'Is it you?' 'I am flattered.'
jokes.setup: Dwight excitedly shows Jim a photo of someone he thinks is Johnny Depp
jokes.punchline: Jim deadpan suggests it might be himself, and is flattered by the comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Jim's deadpan response to Dwight's excitement. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: That's johnny depp. Where'd you take that? In my condo complex. Oh, my god, that's right! I read in people magazine that he was looking for a two-bedroom condo in scranton.
jokes.setup: Dwight shows Jim a photo claiming it's Johnny Depp
jokes.punchline: The absurd idea that a global movie star would live in a Scranton condo complex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight delusion - his gullibility and tendency to believe celebrity sightings. The specificity of 'two-bedroom condo in Scranton' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, jim, jim, Where--where do I find the black pearl? Who's that? Captain jack sparrow. captain jack sparrow, jim. John dillinger. No. I'm captain jack sparrow. Captain crunch.
jokes.setup: Dwight is role-playing as Jack Sparrow
jokes.punchline: Jim's increasingly ridiculous suggestions ending with Captain Crunch
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Jim deflating Dwight's fantasy. Captain Crunch is a nice absurd landing.

jokes.text: Remember how you felt when you thought you'd seen roger clemens? At the yankees game. Yeah. Well, it wasn't him.
jokes.setup: Dwight defending his celebrity sighting by referencing Jim's mistake
jokes.punchline: Jim had to admit his own false celebrity sighting
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing both characters have this flaw, creates equality in their relationship.

jokes.text: It also explains why the name on his mailbox was m. Shulman.
jokes.setup: The Johnny Depp delusion continues
jokes.punchline: Jim points out the obvious flaw in Dwight's theory with perfect deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim delivery - using logic to deflate Dwight's fantasy while staying deadpan.

jokes.text: M. Night shulman?
jokes.setup: Dwight mishears 'M. Shulman'
jokes.punchline: Confuses it with director M. Night Shyamalan
jokes.explanation: Simple mishearing gag that shows Dwight's tendency to connect unrelated things.

jokes.text: First up, the lost and found has gone missing. It itself is lost. So please try not to lose anything until we find it.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing meeting topics
jokes.punchline: The meta-absurdity of the lost and found being lost
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good structure. The redundancy 'it itself is lost' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: You couldn't handle my undivided attention.
jokes.setup: Michael asks for undivided attention
jokes.punchline: Andy's cocky retort suggesting his attention is too powerful
jokes.explanation: Good cocky Andy moment, playing off the Jack Nicholson 'you can't handle the truth' vibe.

jokes.text: Make friends first, make sales second, make love third... In no particular order.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the old office philosophy
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of listing priorities 'in no particular order'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael logic failure. The absurdity is perfectly in character and quotable.

jokes.text: Including the car phone!
jokes.setup: Andy listing all his phone numbers for Michael to reach him
jokes.punchline: The outdated reference to a car phone
jokes.explanation: Minor character beat showing Andy's pretentious outdated tech references.

jokes.text: Oh, you say jesus is king of kings? Well, what does that say to you about how I think of myself?
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming to be king of kings as best salesman
jokes.punchline: Implying he considers himself equal to Jesus
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Dwight moment. Perfect character integration - his ego and religious background colliding in blasphemous absurdity.

jokes.text: Honey, if I don't have time to answer an email, I definitely don't have time to walk over to your desk.
jokes.setup: Pam asks why Jim doesn't answer emails
jokes.punchline: Jim's backwards logic about time management
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about modern workplace logic. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Stop sexting pam.
jokes.setup: Jim is focused on his phone during Andy's congratulations
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate assumption about the texting
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment showing Andy's lack of workplace boundaries.

jokes.text: Honey and jelly sandwich time. You saw who did this, and you didn't stop 'em? Didn't have to see. It was sales--I can feel it.
jokes.setup: Toby's sandwich has been destroyed
jokes.punchline: Toby's mystical ability to sense sales department crimes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Toby's defeated resignation and the absurd 'I can feel it' line.

jokes.text: The sales department smashed my sandwich. Yes. All of 'em. Together. It's a conspiracy.
jokes.setup: Toby explaining his sandwich destruction theory
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of the entire sales team conspiring to destroy one sandwich
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist escalation of the sandwich situation.

jokes.text: That's what she said. Yeah. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Toby says 'You need to get back on top'
jokes.punchline: Michael's automatic 'That's what she said' with Toby's reluctant agreement
jokes.explanation: Standard 'That's what she said' usage. Gets points for Toby's resigned participation.

jokes.text: Hand 'em over, numb nuts. But seriously, I mean, it's your job to give us those leads.
jokes.setup: Andy demanding the leads
jokes.punchline: The immediate pivot from insult to serious business talk
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's fake niceness - insults then immediately goes professional.

jokes.text: Not even sabre owns this place. It's a rental.
jokes.setup: Michael asserting authority over the office
jokes.punchline: The literal truth that deflates his power claim
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Michael's authority with mundane reality. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Okay, just imagine... That instead of going to jail for murdering someone, You got an ice cream cone. If that were the case, then in the summertime, Everyone would go around killing people for--for the pleasure of an ice cream cone.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to explain why rewarding bad behavior is wrong
jokes.punchline: The increasingly dark and absurd analogy about murder incentives
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael absurdist logic. The escalation to seasonal murder sprees is brilliantly dark and unexpected.

jokes.text: Michael, I don't want to 'incentivize' murder.
jokes.setup: Michael's murder/ice cream analogy
jokes.punchline: Gabe's perfectly serious response to the absurd premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. The air quotes around 'incentivize' and treating murder policy as a real concern is great.

jokes.text: So I am going to give the leads to...King creed. And to king...Meredith! And to king angela. Because today we are all kings. And queens.
jokes.setup: Michael required to hand out sales leads
jokes.punchline: Gives them to non-sales people while declaring everyone royalty
jokes.explanation: Good Michael rebellion moment with nice absurdist escalation and inclusive ending

jokes.text: So I am going to give the leads to...King creed. And to king...Meredith! They aren't sales people. And to king angela. Because today we are all kings. And queens.
jokes.setup: Michael threatens to misuse the leads
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical royal distribution system
jokes.explanation: Good Michael power trip absurdity. The royal theme and gender correction at the end work well.

jokes.text: Oh, right. Those are clues. And within each clue is a lesson. You learn a lesson, and then you find a lead.
jokes.setup: Jim discovers the 'leads' aren't actually leads
jokes.punchline: Michael's overcomplicated scavenger hunt explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael over-engineering a simple situation. The mock-profound lesson structure is in character.

jokes.text: Or is it?
jokes.setup: Michael saying 'This one's a map'
jokes.punchline: His mysterious follow-up questioning his own statement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Michael mystery-building. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Oh, how the tables have turned.
jokes.setup: Angela comes to Michael asking for leads
jokes.punchline: Michael's dramatic reversal of fortune declaration
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael dramatic phrase. Gets points for being quotable and in character but lacks originality.

jokes.text: I want to watch the kardashians, okay? I don't want to watch- it's mindless! The kardashians is a good show. Thank you, stanley!
jokes.setup: Angela and others arguing about what to watch
jokes.punchline: Stanley unexpectedly defending the Kardashians
jokes.explanation: Good character surprise - Stanley being the unlikely Kardashian defender against judgmental Angela.

jokes.text: 'when arrogant salesmen are mean to my face, A certain manager will go to his moppy place.' He means his mopey place. It's under that street lamp that he thinks was in casablanca.
jokes.setup: Jim reading Michael's riddle
jokes.punchline: Pam immediately knowing Michael's secret sad place and Casablanca delusion
jokes.explanation: Great character detail about Michael's pathetic habits. Pam's immediate understanding of his psychology is perfect.

jokes.text: God, I love you. Today I turned an office crisis Into a teachable moment. A lesser manager would have screwed this day up royally.
jokes.setup: Pam expressing love for Jim's understanding
jokes.punchline: Cut to Michael's delusional talking head about his management skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect mockumentary editing joke - cutting from genuine emotion to Michael's delusion. Great structural comedy.

jokes.text: Yeah. I basically wish you were dead.
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Michael won't give him leads since he's a jerk salesman
jokes.punchline: Michael's casually delivered death wish
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor delivered with Michael's characteristic casual cruelty. Economy makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I hid the leads. where? Colder. Cold...Er. Warmer. Colder. Colder. C-- Warmer. Warmer... Hot! Hotter... Burning hot! Lower... Are you-- lower... Are you sure? Low...Er...
jokes.setup: Michael making Jim play hot/cold to find leads
jokes.punchline: The extended uncomfortable game with Michael's dramatic temperature calls
jokes.explanation: Extended physical comedy bit. Works for cringe but goes on too long. Michael's dramatic delivery saves it.

jokes.text: The prankster is getting pranked. Ha ha.
jokes.setup: Dwight discovers Jim is stuck in Michael's scavenger hunt
jokes.punchline: His gleeful but awkward declaration of role reversal
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight revenge moment. The mechanical 'Ha ha' makes it slightly funnier.

jokes.text: Where are they? They're in the trash. They're in the trash. the trash. It's code. All right, meredith. Take off your dress. Okey-dokey. No. Dear god, no. It's in the trash can.
jokes.setup: Everyone trying to decode 'trash' as a clue
jokes.punchline: Meredith immediately starting to undress when asked
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect Meredith character moment. Her immediate compliance is both shocking and in character.

jokes.text: If we don't patronize The only syrian restaurant in town, There'll be nothing left but pan pizzas And, you know... Make your own salads.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining why he bought Toby's baba ganoush
jokes.punchline: His concern about Scranton's dining diversity and the dismissive 'make your own salads'
jokes.explanation: Great Kevin moment showing unexpected cultural awareness. The disdain for 'make your own salads' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Tonight is ghost whisperer... So Friday.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what day it is
jokes.punchline: Kevin determining the day by his TV schedule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - using TV schedule as calendar. Shows his priorities and limited thinking.

jokes.text: Michael's elaborate dump trip proposal ending with 'more pizza, call it a night'
jokes.setup: Michael trying to make the dump trip sound fun
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous itinerary including 'more pizza'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael trying to salvage a bad situation. The 'more pizza' detail is perfectly Michael.

jokes.text: Yeah. Well, sometimes the acorn just stays an acorn. If you don't believe me, look in my gutters.
jokes.setup: Dwight's 'acorn becomes the oak' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal deflation with personal home maintenance detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael moment - taking metaphor literally and adding sad personal detail. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Don't use your cute baby to make us like you. She's wearing a onesie. stop it.
jokes.setup: Jim showing baby pictures during the conflict
jokes.punchline: Angela's resistance being worn down by baby cuteness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela character moment - her coldness cracking against baby cuteness. The 'stop it' shows her losing control.

jokes.text: I mean, a lot of their work can be done from india.
jokes.setup: Andy talking about non-sales staff resistance
jokes.punchline: Casually threatening outsourcing as leverage
jokes.explanation: Good dark workplace humor showing Andy's corporate mindset and casual cruelty.

jokes.text: Hey, there is nothing I can do about my wide-set eyes. No, no, no, I'm talking about your personality, dwight!
jokes.setup: Jim calls Dwight a 'wide-eyed innocent'
jokes.punchline: Dwight mishearing as an insult about his eye spacing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Dwight insecurity about his appearance. Great character-driven misunderstanding.

jokes.text: I would be number two right now at a home depot! 'cause they promote from within!
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his alternative career path
jokes.punchline: His passionate defense of Home Depot's promotion policy
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight delusion moment. His genuine pride in corporate advancement potential is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I hitched my wagon To a horse with no legs.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his career disappointment with Michael
jokes.punchline: The vivid metaphor of a legless horse
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor with perfect economy. The image is vivid and the frustration feels real.

jokes.text: Kung fu movies! I--you know what I should have been doing? I should have been out at bars finding my soul mate, Finding my wife, making babies. Nice babies you're making with the floozies at the bars!
jokes.setup: Dwight lamenting wasted time with Jim
jokes.punchline: Jim's savage comeback about bar hookup babies
jokes.explanation: Good escalating argument with Jim getting in a solid burn. Shows both characters' fighting styles.

jokes.text: That's my wife you're talking about, man! Your made-up wife who doesn't exist?
jokes.setup: Dwight defending his hypothetical wife
jokes.punchline: Jim pointing out she's imaginary
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Dwight's delusion and Jim's logic. The passion over an imaginary person is funny.

jokes.text: Erin just texted me back. 'people love shells from faraway beaches.'
jokes.setup: Andy consulting Erin for conflict resolution advice
jokes.punchline: Erin's completely unhelpful beach shell suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin non-sequitur advice. Shows her sweet but completely impractical nature.

jokes.text: No other animal on earth could do this. Maybe beavers... But not like this.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on human accomplishment at the dump
jokes.punchline: His consideration and dismissal of beavers as competition
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael pseudo-profundity. The beaver consideration and qualification is perfectly absurd yet delivered seriously.

jokes.text: ...2% milk! What I forgot for the coffee. Yeah, treats, stanley. They've accepted Our simple offer of treats only, nothing more.
jokes.setup: Jim almost reveals they're giving up commission percentage
jokes.punchline: His quick cover-up about milk and Stanley's satisfied response
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection comedy with Jim's quick thinking and Stanley's easy satisfaction with the explanation.

jokes.text: Just this whole dump, And in the middle, one flower. And the caption would read... Hope... Grows... In a dump.
jokes.setup: Michael describing an artistic photo concept
jokes.punchline: His terrible, cliché inspirational caption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael attempt at profundity. The dramatic delivery of the cliché caption is beautifully cringeworthy.

jokes.text: Eech! I'm never gonna sit on that disgusting seat. Yeah, damn right, you're not, 'cause it's for me and michael only!
jokes.setup: Angela expressing disgust at the dump beanbag chair
jokes.punchline: Dwight treating her rejection as exclusivity rather than disgust
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Dwight's ability to spin rejection as privilege. Perfect misunderstanding of the situation.

jokes.text: You're the nicest person I've ever met.
jokes.setup: Dwight gives Michael his jacket in the cold
jokes.punchline: Michael's sincere gratitude for basic human decency
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending moment showing Michael's low bar for kindness and the reconciliation between him and Dwight.
